sn12.1:1.2: At one time the _Bhagavā_ was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s park.
sn12.1:1.8: Listen to that, attend closely, I will speak.”
sn12.1:2.12: conditioned by birth, old age and death, sorrow, lamentation, _dukkha_, dejection, and distress come to be.
sn12.1:2.13: Thus there is the arising of this whole mass of _dukkha_.
sn12.1:2.5: conditioned by _nāmarūpa_, the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*];
sn12.1:2.6: conditioned by the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*], contact;
sn12.1:3.1: But with the remainderless fading away and cessation of blindness [*avijjā*], there is the cessation of _saṅkhāras_;
sn12.1:3.10: with the cessation of _bhava_, the cessation of birth;
sn12.1:3.11: with the cessation of birth, old age and death, sorrow, lamentation, _dukkha_, dejection, and distress cease.
sn12.1:3.12: Thus there is the cessation of this whole mass of _dukkha_.”
sn12.1:3.14: Satisfied, those _bhikkhus_ delighted in what the _Bhagavā_ had said.
sn12.1:3.2: with the cessation of _saṅkhāras_, the cessation of consciousness [*viññāṇa*];
sn12.1:3.3: with the cessation of consciousness [*viññāṇa*], the cessation of _nāmarūpa_;
sn12.1:3.4: with the cessation of _nāmarūpa_, the cessation of the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*];
sn12.1:3.5: with the cessation of the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*], the cessation of contact;
sn12.1:3.6: with the cessation of contact, the cessation of _vedanā_;
sn12.1:3.7: with the cessation of _vedanā_, the cessation of _taṇhā_;
sn12.1:3.8: with the cessation of _taṇhā_, the cessation of clinging/feeding [*upādāna*];
sn12.1:3.9: with the cessation of clinging/feeding [*upādāna*], the cessation of _bhava_;
